evidence.claim: [MTOR] Positive control performs as expected -> RESCUE. STRING shows direct experimental interactions with RPTOR, RICTOR, RRAGA, RPS6KB1, AKT1, EIF4EBP1, RHEB (all 0.999); OpenTargets places MTOR at the top for the TSC association. Knockdown/inhibition normalizes mTORC1 hyperactivation.

[PSMD13] Exacerbation control confirmed -> WORSEN. mTORC1 hyperactivation in TSC2-null cells causes proteotoxic stress (translation outpaces proteasome clearance); PSMD13 (26S proteasome lid subunit RPN9) knockdown further compromises proteasome capacity, worsening the phenotype.

[CDK9] PRIORITY 1 - strongest mechanistic rescue case. CDK9 is the P-TEFb kinase driving RNA Pol II pause-release; CDK9 physically associates with mTOR-pathway components (Beauchamp et al. 2019, Blood).
[CDK9] Mechanistic cross-talk supporting rescue: mTOR controls P-TEFb activity (mTOR phosphorylates CDK9-Thr186), so the CDK9-mTOR axis is bidirectional.

[FZD7] PRIORITY 2 - best constraint profile. Wnt receptor activating Wnt->Dvl->beta-catenin and Wnt->PI3K->mTOR; rescue rationale is that FZD7 ligands (Wnt5A, Wnt7A) activate PI3K-AKT upstream of TSC2/mTOR, so knockdown could dampen mTOR drive.

[NCOA6] Indirect PI3K-mTOR link but highly constrained. NCOA6 phospho-Ser884 promotes PI3K/AKT/mTOR signalling in ER+ breast cancer; knockdown could reduce mTOR-pathway transcription, but gnomAD pLI=1.000 flags tolerability risk.

[BCL2] Low priority - downstream effector. mTOR hyperactivation drives BCL2 upregulation via survival signalling, so BCL2 is a consequence rather than a driver; BCL2 knockdown restores apoptotic sensitivity but does not address upstream mTORC1.
evidence.source: EMET pathway reasoning (mTOR -> BCL2 survival axis)

evidence.claim: [KMT2D] Mechanistically contradictory + dangerously constrained -> predicted to WORSEN. KMT2D drives SMG1 (an mTOR-kinase antagonist) expression via H3K4Me1 deposition; KMT2D knockdown would lower SMG1 and thereby increase mTOR activity.

[SMARCE1] Predicted to WORSEN. In clear cell meningioma, SMARCE1 LOSS activates mTOR; therefore SMARCE1 knockdown would worsen mTORC1 hyperactivation. pLI=1.000, LOEUF=0.376; Coffin-Siris syndrome gene.

[RPS3] ABSOLUTE EXCLUSION - pan-essential / knockdown lethal. CCLE log2=11.88 (highest in panel; expressed in 1,654/1,673 lines), GTEx max brain TPM=175, pLI=1.000, LOEUF=0.244; ribosomal-protein knockdown would collapse translation.

[ACTR3] EXCLUSION - universal pan-essential / knockdown lethal. Expressed in all 54 GTEx tissues and all 1,673 CCLE lines, pLI=1.000; Arp3 knockdown impairs cytokinesis and endocytosis globally; actin remodelling is downstream, not an mTOR driver.
[DIDO1] EXCLUDE - no mTOR connection. pLI=1.000, LOEUF=0.244; H3K4me3 reader / RNA splicing; myeloproliferative-neoplasm disease gene; no TSC2/mTOR link.

[SAP18] EXCLUDE - CNS confounder. Highest CNS expression in the panel (spinal cord TPM=205, substantia nigra=113) with ZERO mTOR-pathway connections; a neurotoxic knockdown phenotype would confound any rescue signal in neural TSC2 models.

[VPS54] LOW / EXCLUDE - link too indirect. pLI=0.991; STRING connections exclusively to the GARP complex (VPS53, VPS51, VPS52) with no mTOR-pathway genes at any confidence; the lysosome->Rag-GTPase->mTOR link is too indirect and non-selective.
[DPM2] NO MECHANISM. The most gnomAD-tolerant gene in the panel (pLI=0.011, LOEUF=1.190) but with no TSC2/mTOR mechanistic basis; biallelic mutations cause CDG-Ih (OpenTargets genetic score 0.819).

[SSU72] NO LINK / possible artifact. pLI=0.017 (tolerant), ~38 TPM average in brain, but no TSC2/mTOR connection in STRING, Reactome, or any literature found - may represent a screen artifact or off-target.
